statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with 19 of 27 countries showing critical status in at least one fuel category, despite overall reserves above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure across multiple countries and fuel types, though aggregate EU reserves remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum. While EU averages show 113 days for petrol, 102.2 days for diesel, and 96.4 days for jet fuel, the distribution reveals concerning vulnerabilities. Romania presents the most severe situation with all three fuel types in critical status, including dangerously low diesel reserves at just 30.2 days.

Diesel emerges as the most problematic fuel category, with 14 of 27 countries showing critical or warning status, including major economies like Germany (64.3 days) and Spain (68.6 days). Jet fuel reserves are similarly strained, with 19 countries below safe levels and several showing extremely low stocks—Poland at 25.3 days and Portugal at 26.7 days pose particular concern given their strategic importance.

The fuel price landscape reflects underlying supply tensions, with Brent crude at $103.54 per barrel—a 42.9% increase since the late-February Middle East escalation. Consumer prices show significant variation across the bloc, with Netherlands petrol reaching €2.388 per liter while Malta maintains €1.34, highlighting different national tax policies and supply chain pressures. The combination of elevated crude prices and uneven reserve distribution suggests continued vulnerability to supply disruptions.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries show critical status in at least one fuel category, with Romania facing critical levels across all three fuel types
Diesel reserves are most concerning, with 14 countries showing critical or warning status including Germany (64.3 days) and Spain (68.6 days)
Jet fuel stocks are severely strained in 19 countries, with Poland (25.3 days) and Portugal (26.7 days) showing extremely low reserves
Brent crude has surged 42.9% since late February to $103.54/barrel, driving consumer fuel prices to €1.82-2.39 per liter across the EU
Despite individual country vulnerabilities, EU aggregate reserves remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum for all fuel types
